_comment: Canonical Tribunal model. Sage (the primary LLM) never proposes shell commands; Sage sends the Tribunal a natural-language `request` plus optional `guidelines`, and the Tribunal is the sole authority for producing the final command string. There is no fallback — when the Tribunal fails, the tool call fails.
name: Tribunal
description: Command authority. Converts Sage's natural-language request + guidelines into a precise shell command through multi-perspective voting and verification.

reasoning.description: The logic behind the member's verdict or revision.

command.description: The proposed command string for this member.

request.description: Sage's natural-language description of what the Operator must accomplish. No shell syntax.

guidelines.description: Optional plain-language creative guidance for the Tribunal (flags to favour, behaviours to avoid, preferred output format). No shell syntax.

vote_winner.description: The command that won the weighted majority vote.

outcome.description: Terminal outcome of the pipeline. Only successful outcomes are enumerated; Tribunal failures raise typed TribunalError subclasses instead of producing a fallback result.
